pageTitle.value: Explore remote volcanoes affecting earth’s climate - Video

pageCaption.value: Explore remote volcanoes affecting earth’s climate

WaPageTitle.value: Explore remote volcanoes affecting earth’s climate - Video

metadescription.value: Explore remote volcanoes affecting earth’s climate

previewTitle.value: Explore remote volcanoes affecting earth’s climate - Video

text.value: French volcanologist Yves Moussallam wants to shed light on a great scientific unknown: how the gases and aerosols emitted by the Earth’s 150-plus active volcanoes are affecting climate change.

pageCaption.value: Vreni Häusserman is a German biologist who is exploring the unique marine life of the Patagonian fjords in the south of Chile. These extraordinary and delicate underwater environments are suffering from the effects of intensive fish farming and global warming. Vreni is planning ever-deeper dives to study, understand and preserve this unique biodiversity before it is too late.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 1

pageCaption.value: “Think like an ocean”. Working to protect the iconic Galápagos Islands Marine Reserve, Sylvia Earle and her Mission Blue team led a groundbreaking expedition to assess the health of the ocean after nearly 25 years of protection. What they found could be used as a blueprint of success for the rest of the planet.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 1

metadescription.value: 25 years after the creation of the Galápagos Marine Reserve, an expedition has been launched to collect new data. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 2

pageCaption.value: Mission Blue has been helping to track animals in the Galápagos Marine Reserve to analyse their well-being around the archipelago. Through this work, legendary oceanographer Sylvia Earle and a team of scientists are making incredible insights into the connectivity of the Galápagos with the rest of the planet – including discovering DNA sequences that could be completely new to science.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 2

metadescription.value: Using eDNA, researchers are aiming to characterize the marine diversity of the Galápagos to protect specific habitats. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Electronic ears to listen to the rainforests - Video

pageCaption.value: Technologist Topher White is giving scientists and conservationists a unique view of wildlife in the world’s rainforests – through an alert system he originally developed to detect illegal logging using old mobile phones.

WaPageTitle.value: Electronic ears to listen to the rainforests - Video

metadescription.value: Technologist Topher White is giving scientists and conservationists a unique view of wildlife in the world’s rainforests – through an alert system he originally developed to detect illegal logging using old mobile phones.

previewTitle.value: Electronic ears to listen to the rainforests - Video

pageCaption.value: Andrew Bastawrous is an ophthalmologist from England whose dream is to treat the millions of visually impaired people living in low-income countries. He has begun a project in remote areas of Kenya, where there is little eye care but good mobile phone technology. His solution is to use smartphones as a portable eye examination kit, and eventually make this technology available around the world.

pageCaption.value: Glacial microbiologist Joseph Cook likens the surface of Greenland’s ice sheet to a “frozen rainforest”. The top few metres of ice are a biological realm whose dimensions, role and impacts are still a scientific mystery. Cook’s Ice Alive mission aims to reveal how this ecosystem helps drive changes in the Earth’s climate, nutrient and carbon cycles, which in turn affect humanity.
